data_sources: Granola (GLOC, CLS Health, Tenet Health, Premier Ortho, OS1, GSD War Rooms, Monarch Reviews — meeting notes through May 1, 2026)
notes: No Slack or Gong data available in this run — based on Granola meeting notes only. Data covers Apr 3–May 1, 2026.

excluded_deals.name: Plymouth Bay Ortho
excluded_deals.reason: No-go confirmed Apr 22 — requires Maya/ClearPay integration they don't want; ECW build requirements unclear; won't build custom solution for single urgent care location

excluded_deals.name: Remix Medical
excluded_deals.reason: Closed out 4/7 — unresponsive since Feb 25 across multiple follow-ups; Osvaldo was not the DM, providers never engaged

excluded_deals.name: Hemorrhoid Centers of America
excluded_deals.reason: Passed 3/31 — Dr. Chrono EHR incompatibility; outside ICP; price-sensitive

excluded_deals.name: Total Primary Care
excluded_deals.reason: Cut off 4/7 — won't replace CTO-built scheduler; Primary Care Connect not on Monarch roadmap for 6–9 months

deals.name: GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)
deals.size: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.40/visit (ClearPay); 2 pilot locations (5 total post-merger); 14 physicians + ~40 PAs; ~750 calls/week/location
deals.status_narrative: Signed Apr 27 — first true Monarch win. Internal EPD kickoff with Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, Carli, and Erik held May 1. Implementation sequence: voice agent setup → 30-day listen mode → 60-day development → commercial launch. No payment until Phase 3. GLOC's expansion pipeline will triple the org (14 → 50 physicians, two large clinics acquired). Leadership team (including Jim) is on vacation until ~May 16 and unavailable for external kickoff until then.
key_risks: Leadership unavailable until May 16 — listen mode setup delayed
Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) unproven at scale
Org just 3x'd via acquisitions — implementation surface area elevated
deals.next_step: Brantley to contact Jim for IT contact (Athena login + Zoom phones confirmation) and schedule external kickoff for week of May 19. Owner: Brantley. Due: Week of May 5.
deals.next_step_timing: Outreach week of May 5; external kickoff ~May 19
deals.key_notes_one_line: SIGNED 4/27; internal EPD kickoff done 5/1; external kickoff blocked until Jim returns ~May 16.

deals.name: CLS Health
deals.size: 150–200 primary care physicians (Houston TX); 15–30 telehealth visits/day to start (~1,200/month); scaling toward 1,000 providers
deals.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Mgr, internal champion); Ammar Baba (Director, exec sponsor); CLS President engaged and applying internal pressure
deals.status_narrative: Two-part deal: (1) UC Telehealth — ramp pricing approved by Teresa 4/29; (2) Specialties — Kapeesh scoping call needed, position as design partner. CLS pushed back Apr 23 on flat $18K over 6 months before service line revenue — requested hybrid/per-encounter pricing, 6-month initial term, and waived $2K implementation fee. Teresa approved ramp pricing direction for UC only, with stronger design partner incentives for specialties. Executive call with Solv COO/CPO being set for week of May 4. Target launch June 1. ECW + SolvPay compatibility is the single question gating Part 1 entirely.
key_risks: ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — this single question gates the entire Part 1 green-light
Pricing concession stack (ramp, 6-month term, waived impl fee) compresses margin; needs finance sign-off
EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year — CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
deals.next_step: Send updated proposal to CLS with ramp pricing (UC telemed) + design partner framing (specialties). Set COO/CPO exec call for week of May 4. Confirm ECW + SolvPay compatibility before proposal. Owner: Brantley/Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: Immediately.
deals.next_step_timing: Proposal immediate; exec call week of May 4
deals.key_notes_one_line: Ramp + design partner approach approved 4/29; exec COO/CPO call being set for May 4 week; ECW+SolvPay compat is the blocker.

deals.name: Premier Ortho
deals.size: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; pricing modeled at $0.60–$1.00/resolved call
deals.champion: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and proactive; Dr. Basmajian (CEO, ultimate DM — never directly engaged)
deals.status_narrative: Paused since 3/11. Jon (COO) proactively communicated Asort Health contractual obligations block all movement — 'our hands are tied at the moment' — no exit timeline given. Deal was at near-Paper Process (DocuSign ready) before the Asort blocker surfaced Feb 25. Jon confirmed intent to return when clear. Brantley check-in cadence is now overdue (~4/14 was last target).
key_risks: Asort contract exit has no timeline — fully outside Solv's control
Check-in now overdue; competitive exposure if Asort resolves and another vendor stays active
Dr. Basmajian (ultimate DM) has never been directly engaged by Solv
deals.next_step: Brantley to send passive check-in to Jon — targeted 4/14 cadence is overdue. Monitor only; do not push. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Asort contract hard block since 3/11; Jon intends to return when clear; check-in overdue.

deals.name: OS1 Orthopedic & Sports Injury Clinics
deals.size: 4 locations; cash-constrained (opened 3 clinics simultaneously); Atlanta/Kennesaw location struggling
deals.champion: Randy Johansen (Board Member, former CEO of AFC; intro via Heather at UCA)
deals.status_narrative: Warm post-UCA intro via Heather. First substantive call Apr 24. Randy was meeting with OS1 team ~Apr 29–30 to assess appetite for reviewing solutions. Reconnect with Brantley expected week of May 1–5. Cash-strapped flag is serious — could kill the deal before it starts. Monthly board cadence means slow decision cycles. Evan Cory preparing Maya and ClearPay one-pager collateral.
key_risks: Cash constraints may prevent near-term close regardless of fit
Monthly board cadence means 30+ day cycle per decision
No EHR confirmed — integration scope unknown
deals.next_step: Brantley reconnect call with Randy after his OS1 internal meeting (expected this week). Share one-pager when ready. Owner: Brantley / Evan. Due: Week of May 4.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Post-UCA board intro; OS1 internal review ~Apr 29–30; reconnect with Brantley expected week of May 4.

deals.name: Tenet Health (Physician Group)
deals.size: — (scope pending NDA and scoping docs); physician group side only (Tenet exited UC)
deals.status_narrative: New inbound RFP as of May 1. Tenet replacing IVR/phone tree system across physician group — Maya is the right product. Already 4 vendors in process with initial proposals submitted; Solv added as 5th late. Andy Salkin intro email to Brantley pending (expected Monday May 4). NDA template from Nick expected by end of May 1 (may slip to Monday). Full RFP and scoping docs shared after NDA. Decision targeting 'this year' but realistically Q1 2027.
key_risks: 4 competitors have a head start with initial proposals already submitted
Decision timeline Q1 2027 — long cycle with no close-date urgency
Scope and location count unknown until NDA + scoping docs received
deals.next_step: Andy Salkin intro email to Brantley (Monday May 4); Brantley sign NDA and receive scoping docs; begin RFP response. Owner: Andy / Brantley. Due: NDA by May 5.
deals.key_notes_one_line: New May 1 RFP entry; Maya IVR replacement; 4 competitors ahead; Q1 2027 timeline.

deals.name: Elevate Health Group
deals.deal_type: Expansion (inferred — Dr. Tovmassian also owns Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care)
deals.size: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; SoCal; ClearPay only
deals.champion: Katia Sarafian (Admin, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit product-fit objection
deals.status_narrative: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian (owner) pushed back explicitly on 3/16: 'This doesn't make sense for our practice. 90% of our patients are recurring and their insurances are stable.' ClearPay's point-of-care estimate model doesn't fit a high-recurring primary care patient mix. No response to follow-ups since 3/24 — dark 5+ weeks. Aggressive pursuit risks the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership.
key_risks: Product-fit objection is explicit and owner-level — not pricing or timing
Dark since mid-March; 5+ weeks of ghosting
Aggressive pursuit risks the existing Mederi UC partnership
deals.next_step: One final light-touch follow-up only. No active pursuit. Re-engage only if ClearPay evolves for recurring primary care. Owner: Brantley.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Owner's explicit product-fit objection since 3/16; near-dead; pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership.

executive_summaries.gloc:
**GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)** — Closed Won | Design Partner
- **Size**: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.40/visit (ClearPay); 2 pilot locations (5 total post-merger); 14 physicians + ~40 PAs; ~750 calls/wk/location
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: James Stilley (CEO)
- **Status**: Signed Apr 27 — first Monarch win. Internal EPD kickoff (Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, Carli, Erik) completed May 1. Implementation: 30-day listen mode → 60-day dev → commercial launch. No payment until Phase 3. Expansion pipeline will triple org to 50 physicians via two acquisitions. Leadership on vacation until ~May 16.
- **Key risks**:
  - Leadership unavailable until May 16 — listen mode setup delayed
  - Ortho scheduling complexity unproven at scale
  - 3x org growth via acquisitions elevates implementation complexity
- **Next step**: Brantley contact Jim for IT person (Athena login + Zoom phones) and schedule external kickoff ~May 19. Due: Week of May 5.
executive_summaries.cls_health:
**CLS Health** — Paper Process | New Logo + Design Partner
- **Size**: 150–200 providers; 15–30 telehealth visits/day to start; Houston TX; scaling to 1,000 providers
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops); Ammar Baba (Director, exec); CLS President engaged
- **Status**: Ramp pricing + design partner approach approved by Teresa Apr 29. Two-part structure: (1) UC telemed with ramp pricing, (2) specialties as design partner with stronger incentives. CLS pushed back on flat $18K over 6 months — requested hybrid model, 6-month initial term, waived $2K impl fee. Executive call with Solv COO/CPO being set for May 4 week. Target launch June 1. ECW + SolvPay compatibility is the single question gating the entire deal.
- **Key risks**:
  - ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — gates Part 1 entirely
  - Pricing concession stack compresses margin; needs finance sign-off
  - EMR switch to Epic/Oracle in ~1 year; CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
- **Next step**: Send updated proposal (ramp UC + design partner specialties). Confirm ECW+SolvPay compat first. Set COO/CPO exec call for May 4 week. Owner: Brantley/Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: Immediately.
executive_summaries.premier_ortho:
**Premier Ortho** — Clear MSP (Formally Paused) | New Logo
- **Size**: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; modeled at $0.60–$1.00/resolved call
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jon Borja (COO), proactive and transparent
- **Status**: Paused since 3/11. Jon communicated Asort contract blocks all movement — no timeline for exit. Deal was near-Paper Process (DocuSign ready) before blocker surfaced. Jon confirmed intent to return. Brantley check-in cadence now overdue from ~4/14 target.
- **Key risks**:
  - Asort exit fully outside Solv's control — no active path forward
  - Check-in overdue — competitive exposure if Asort resolves
  - Dr. Basmajian (ultimate DM) has never been directly engaged
- **Next step**: Brantley passive check-in to Jon this week. Monitor only. Owner: Brantley.

Cash-strapped flag is serious — opened 3 clinics simultaneously, Atlanta/Kennesaw struggling. Monthly board cadence slows decisions. Evan building Maya + ClearPay one-pager collateral.

**Tenet Health (Physician Group)** — Prospecting | New Logo

- **Status**: New RFP entry as of May 1. Tenet replacing IVR/phone tree with AI agent — Maya is the right fit. Already 4 vendors with initial proposals submitted; Solv added 5th and late. Andy Salkin intro email to Brantley expected May 4. NDA pending. Decision targeting Q1 2027.

executive_summaries.elevate_health_group:
**Elevate Health Group** — Prospecting (Near Dead) | Expansion
- **Size**: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; ClearPay only
- **EHR**: ECW (inferred)
- **Champion**: Katia Sarafian (Admin); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit objection
- **Status**: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian pushed back Mar 16 — '90% of patients recurring, doesn't make sense.' Dark since mid-March. Pursuing risks the Mederi UC partnership.

  - Pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership
